title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
summary: Kagenti is a security platform for multi-agent AI systems that uses identity-based security, delegation chains, and open source infrastructure to prevent data leaks and unauthorized access in agentic AI deployments. The approach emphasizes zero-trust principles and identity management for coordinating multiple AI agents in production environments.
writeup: This video covers Kagenti, a security platform for multi-agent AI systems built on identity-based security, delegation chains, and open source infrastructure. The focus is preventing data leaks and unauthorized access in agentic AI deployments by applying zero-trust principles and identity management to coordinate multiple agents in production. Key takeaways: agent coordination is treated as an identity and access problem, and delegation chains govern how permissions pass between agents during execution.
